summary.estimated_reduction: N/A — no prompt documentation files in changed-files set
summary.note: All three changed files are bash shell hook scripts (.sh), not markdown prompt/command/agent documentation files (.md). The prompt-inspector's detection rules target markdown verbose section headers (## Philosophy, ## Overview, ## Quality Standards, ## Safety Features, ## Helper Scripts, ## Usage) which do not apply to shell scripts. Comments in these files are implementation-level code comments explaining security logic, not verbose non-functional documentation prose.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed the zero-findings draft is correct. Filed one OBSERVATION_ONLY (no false positives/missed verbosity/severity errors) and one actionable PROPOSED_FIX recommending files_inspected: 0 with a skipped_non_scope_files array.

findings.issue: No false positives, missed critical verbosity, or severity misclassification found. The zero-findings draft is correct because the changed-files set contains only .sh hook scripts, while prompt-inspector scope is command/agent Markdown prompt docs and Markdown verbose-section patterns.
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed the draft findings are correct. No action needed.

findings.issue: If the final JSON summary counts these shell scripts as inspected prompt files, that would be misleading; they should be treated as out-of-scope/non-eligible inputs.
findings.proposed_fix: Report files_inspected: 0 for prompt-doc files, and optionally add skipped_non_scope_files array listing the three .sh files.
findings.rationale: Applied. Report now uses files_inspected: 0 and includes skipped_non_scope_files array with all three .sh files listed with reasons.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied codex suggestion: files_inspected set to 0 (not 3) and added skipped_non_scope_files array documenting why each .sh file was excluded from prompt verbosity analysis.
